research_note.summary: With thinking enabled and a reasoning-aware 12000-token cap, the grounded long-context contract completed naturally and answered the pilot 002 question positively: locator support validity recovered from 0/4 to 8/9 while abstraction breadth grew to three conditional principles over nine faithful claims. Residual weaknesses are entry-cap adherence, flat confidence calibration, and one off-by-one locator.
what_worked: Reasoning headroom converted the grounded long-context contract from structurally failing (pilots 002/003) to complete and naturally finished.
Locator pointing recovered to 8/9 support-valid at finer claim granularity than pilot 006's 3/3 on the same source.
Abstraction quality held while breadth improved: three principles with preserved conditionality, supported-claim citations, and self-declared limitations.
The model's own method-failure notes identified real source pathologies (repeated transcript lines, mid-sentence excerpt ending) confirmed independently by strict review.
One accepted span (SLC-001, lines 12-18) reproduced pilot 006's accepted span byte-for-byte, giving cross-pilot locator consistency.
what_failed: Entry caps were ignored: nine claims and locators against an at-most-four contract; thinking mode favored completeness over contract adherence.
Locator confidence calibration is unusable: near-flat 1.0 values while strict review demoted five exact labels to approximate.
One locator missed by one line (the look-below-and-fail symmetry claim), showing exact boundary emission is still imperfect.
The zero-entry unsupported-claim report cannot be distinguished from an unexamined one.
negative_results: The pilot 002 cap-pressure confound is resolved in the practical sense: with headroom and thinking, single-pass grounded long-context works; the earlier locator collapse was a budget phenomenon, not an inherent single-pass limit on this excerpt.
Pilot 003's empty-content lesson stands: the gains here required the reasoning-aware cap.
reusable_by_other_labs: Thinking-enabled runs need caps sized for reasoning plus contract; record reasoning and content tokens separately.
Treat emitted locator confidence as decoration until calibration is demonstrated; strict review labels are the usable signal.
Entry caps in prompt contracts are soft constraints under thinking mode; enforce them in local validation rather than relying on the model.
Line-range-first plus local offset/hash computation transferred cleanly from the chunked lab to the long-context lab, supporting the method lesson's cross-method generality.
do_not_repeat: Do not treat 8/9 on one excerpt as repeated evidence; this is one run on one source in one lab.
Do not use emitted confidence values as acceptance criteria.
Do not relax local entry-cap validation because content quality was high this time.
Do not copy raw source text, raw model output, reasoning traces, provider payloads, or prompt traces into committed records or currentness docs.
research_note.evidence_disclaimer: This is a proposal-only live LLM pilot record, not validation, product evidence, strategy evidence, financial advice, live-trading authority, or architecture.
